content:
I just adapted my Solver to meet CodinGame's timeout criteria (for personnal usage my Solver is more tolerant to timeouts). It actually scored 112 points over the 10 validators. A low victory result leaves your character with ~2 hp while a high victory result leaves him with ~20 hp.

The maximum score should be around 200.
It is a good idea.

Some general comments first before I take a more in-depth review: * Formatting of actions: Please use and tags, e.g. MOVE dir. * Formatting of HP and AP values: Consider formatting the values using , e.g. 40 HP. * End conditions: Please use separate sections for Victory and Defeat. You may refer to the page below as a reference: https://www.codingame.com/ide/puzzle/git-patchwork * Input sections: Please add "space-separated" or "separated by a space" to all applicable lines. * Constraints: Please show one constraint per line. * Cover picture: Consider adding a cover picture for the puzzle. * Viewer: Are you going to add an opening sequence? Thanks.

Hello,

For some reason the opening demo was working with the Main test class provided, but it seems not to be working with the exported project.

I have to investigate further in order to understand why.

I took into account most of your feedback about the statement. I hope it's better now.

I fixed the Demo that was not showing. But surprisingly, the Referee seems not to be able to output within the 10000ms alloted time.

I can't find yet why it is working to generate the Demo but not to allow a player on the platform to run a test case...

Another puzzle ( https://www.codingame.com/contribute/view/101457c36421540967e67263c5d29e9a89179a ) once suffered from a large replay size, but I don't know if that's the case here. Just offering a wild guess.

I'm not an expert here, so if necessary you may seek advice on CodinGame's Discord chat.

I'll review your contribution again within the next few days.

Found the answer on the discord. The gameengine version was too low (4.0.0 instead of 4.5.0) and the score parsing function migrated from a string parameter to a double paremeter, making the compilation of the Referee crash.

It's all working now. :)

Glad to hear that the issue was resolved quickly! Below are some suggestions and clarification questions after reviewing the statement. Goal * Please change "6 first turns" to "first 6 turns". Rules - Resources * Please change "per turn, restored" to "initially, and AP is restored in full". Rules - Available actions * Please add a blank line before the heading "Available actions". * Please change "" to "dir". * In PASS, please add "pending" after "turns". * The statements • They can also perform at most 2 jumps per turn. • Each turn, you send exactly one action. appear contradictory. The former implies that multiple actions per turn are possible (two JUMP actions), while the latter suggests only one action per turn. Please clarify the intended behaviour. Rules - Effects of a movement * Please change "Any movement costs 1 HP" to "The character moves and loses 1 HP." * Does this mean that if a character with 1 HP moves onto a cell containing a frame, they would die (because movement costs 1 HP) before the HP cost is refunded (if applicable)? Please clarify the behaviour. * If the bullet points describe the exact sequence of events, please add at the beginning: The following events happen in the following order: Otherwise please arrange the bullet points in the correct order and add the above text. * Please add ", keeping the same relative direction from the character" after "pushed one cell away from the character", if that accurately describes the push behaviour. * Please clarify under what conditions a flame cannot move. * Please clarify how attraction works when a flame isn't aligned with one of the eight compass directions relative to the character. Which cell does it move to in that case? * Is it possible for two flames to be pushed or attracted to the same cell? If so, is overlap allowed, or are neither moved? Please clarify the intended behaviour. Rules - Flame spawning * Please change "6 first turns" to "first 6 turns". * "glyphs appear": Do you mean "new glyphs appear"? * Consider removing the middle sentence describing how the referee chooses glyph sequences, since the selection mechanism doesn't affect player decisions; players get the pending glyph positions each turn. Of course, if the intention is for players to study the referee logic for offline optimisation, then I'll discuss with you how that should be mentioned or handled further. * Please clarify whether pending glyphs for a turn appear in the immediate next turn. Missing rules * The mechanism describing how IMMO affects flame movement is currently unclear. Please consider adding a section explaining this behaviour in greater detail. * Please specify the coordinate system used in the grid: • Is (0, 0) located at the top-left or bottom-left corner? • In which directions do the x and y coordinates increase? (reached character limit…)

(…continued)

Defeat conditions / Constraints
* If glyphs only convert to flames when PASS is executed, a player could theoretically avoid activating them by never passing. Is there a maximum number of turns or another mechanic preventing this? If so, please add the maximum number of turns to the Constraints section and include a relevant defeat condition.

Initialization input
* Line 1: Please add "space-separated" before "integers".

* Please change "The next" to "Next".

* Please add "of length <var>width</var>" after "a string".

Input for each turn
* Please change "The next" to "Next" (twice).

* Please add "space-separated" before "integers":
  • Line 1
  • Line 2
  • Next flameCount lines
  • Next glyphCount lines

Output
* Please change "<dir>" to "dir".

Constraints
* Please merge the constraints for charX, flameX and glyphX:

0 ≤ <var>charX</var>, <var>flameX</var>, <var>glyphX</var> < <var>width</var>

* Similarly, please merge the constraints for charY, flameY and glyphY.

Cover picture
* Consider adding a cover picture for the puzzle.

Cases and testing
* I'll review these later.

Thanks.

I updated the statements, taking most of your reviews into account. I am now allowing players to output several actions in the same turn, but only allowing one "PASS" at most (in order to provide the next glyphs coordinates).

Furthermore, death is no longer a "lose" if the actions were valid. It is instead a "win" but with a score of 0.

About the maximum amount of turns : the game is designed so that the 200 turn limit (default set) shall never be reached, because the character would die way before that. PASSing will make the character lose 1 hp over time, and moving without extinguishing flames have the same result. Overall, the turn 80 should never be reached.

Moreover, I now list in the input a list of valid actions to the user so that it's easier to develop a working program.

Thanks for the review ! Feel free to check if the updated statements is now more accurate.
Thank you! I should be able to get back to you next Monday or Tuesday.

The revised statement is much clearer now! Here are my comments on the updated contribution: Goal * Please change "6 first turns" to "first 6 turns". * 3rd paragraph: Please format "HP" using the tag. Rules - Coordinate system * If applicable, consider stating that the map boundaries are always walls. This would avoid having to specify what happens when the character or a flame attempts to move outside the map. * Consider renaming this section as "Map". * Consider moving this section before "Resources", since it may be more natural to introduce where entities are located before explaining how they move. Rules - Flame spawning * Consider moving this section before "Resources", as it explains when glyphs appear, which could serve as foundation information before describing how they turn into flames and interact with the character. * The heading "Flame spawning" may be slightly misleading, since this section actually describes when glyphs appear, rather than when flames appear. You might consider renaming it to something like "Glyph spawning" or "Glyph appearance". * The term "pending glyph" may be unclear. Consider defining it here or under "Available actions" as a glyph that has appeared but has not yet turned into a flame. Rules - Resources * To avoid repeating explanations that appear later, you might consider rewriting this section as follows. Note that the last sentence about jumps per turn is removed since it does not directly relate to resources. Your character starts with 40 HP and 10 AP (Action Points). HP can be refunded and AP can be restored (see details below). Rules - Available actions * IMMO: Consider replacing "flames toward a diagonally adjacent cell" with "1 cell in the given diagonal direction". * PASS: I'm not sure I understand the relevance of a jump counter, since at most two jumps are already allowed per turn. * PASS: Please change "turns" to "converts" to avoid misreading "turns" as a noun. * Consider removing "Maximum 2 jumps per turn." from the JUMP description and instead adding a short paragraph at the end of this section to summarise the action limits (please correct this if I misunderstood): Each turn, subject to AP availability, you may perform any number of MOVE and IMMO and at most 2 JUMP. You can also perform one PASS. Rules - Effects of a movement (MOVE or JUMP) * Before describing the sequence of events, it may help to clarify where the character is allowed to move or jump. For example: • Can the character move or jump only to open cells? • Are moves into walls or out-of-bounds cells impossible? • Is moving onto a cell occupied by a glyph allowed? • For JUMP, can the character jump over a wall, or must both cells in the jumping direction be open? (reached character limit…)

(…continued) * point 2: Please format "HP" using the tag. * point 3: Please add "or" before "already". * point 4: Consider rewriting the last sentence for clarity: If any flame is attracted to the same cell as the character, the character dies instantly. Rules - Effects of IMMO dir * Please remove "dir" from the heading. * point 2: Consider rewriting the last sentence for clarity: Unlike movement, any flame attracted to the same cell as the character does not cause instant death. * Just for my curiosity: can the character continue to stay at the same location as a flame after the first IMMO, perform PASS or more IMMO, and still be unharmed by the flame? Rules - End of turn (PASS) * "If there are pending glyphs, they turn into active flames on all cells not occupied by the character or another active flame.": This reads as if flames would appear on nearly every empty cell on the map. Should "on all cells" be changed to "at their locations if the cell is"? All sections * The term "active flame(s)" appears several times. If there's only one type of flame (i.e. a flame simply exists until extinguished), then consider simplifying "active flame(s)" to just "flame(s)". Input for each turn * Does "pending glyphs this turn" refer to "only the new glyphs appearing this turn" or "all pending glyphs, including existing ones and new ones that appear this turn"? Please clarify the wording. * "jumpsLeft" seems irrelevant as explained in my comment above and may be removed. (It's always 2 per turn.) Output for each turn * Please change "a PASS" to "the first PASS". Constraints * "jumpsLeft" seems irrelevant as explained in my comment above and may be removed. (It's always 2 per turn.) Cases and testing * As there are still a number of details to clarify, I'll review these later. Thanks.

I updated the statement with your comments, and also added some illustrations to make it easier to understand the push/attract mechanisms.

The Map section now specify that the map is static over the different tests (but the glyphs are not, they depend on the seed used to generate the next states). I also added that the borders are walls (so it's not possible to go out of the map).

I simplified the notions of "pending glyphs" and "active flames" :
- A glyph is a pending flame
- A flame is a glyph that was converted into a flame
- So there is in fact no need to add an adjective to qualify these entities. I removed the "pending" and "active" words everywhere.

There was a confusion between a "Character's turn" and a "CodinGame's turn". A "Character's turn" is all the actions he does until he uses "PASS". A "CodinGame's turn" is all the actions the player did output in the game loop, and he is not compelled to end a "CodinGame's turn" with a "PASS". The jump counter is in fact the number of times the player can use the "JUMP" action before the next "PASS" action. So it is relevant to give this information as an input so the player can synchronise with the Referee's state.

You asked a series of questions that should be answered in the revised statement :
Q: Can the character move or jump only to open cells?
A: He can move or jump on any cell that is not a wall.
Q: Are moves into walls or out-of-bounds cells impossible?
A: Yes, they are impossible. Out-of-bounds cells are not even reachable given the static Map layout because the borders are a two-wide wall.
Q: Is moving onto a cell occupied by a glyph allowed?
A: Yes, see answer to first question
Q: For JUMP, can the character jump over a wall, or must both cells in the jumping direction be open?
A: The JUMP action can be over a wall or any other entity. The intermediate cell is not even checked when a JUMP action is called. It's like a teleportation.
Q: can the character continue to stay at the same location as a flame after the first IMMO, perform PASS or more IMMO, and still be unharmed by the flame?
A: the character or a flame can go over a glyph, but there can only be one of those entity on a given cell. Using "IMMO" that would make a flame collide with the character instead let the flame where it was (the flame does not move because it is blocked by the character).

About the test cases :
- They are very similar, the map layout is always the same, and the glyphs spawn on fixed locations
- The only thing that changes over different test cases is the seed used to pick a state that yields glyph's spawn positions
- The glyph's spawn mechanism is based on what is done in the Dofus game (see the credits in the goal section)

Thanks for the latest revision and explanation. The statement is becoming much clearer. Below are my suggestions on this version.

All sections
* The term "active flames" still appears in three places. Please remove the word "active" in those places.

* As you pointed out earlier, I had confused the two notions of turns. To avoid misunderstanding, I suggest using "cycle" or "game cycle" instead (for simplicity, I'll use "cycle" in the comments below):

  • After the "Map" section, consider adding a section titled "Cycle" explaining that the character may perform several actions in a cycle, and every cycle ends with a <action>PASS</action>.

  • All mentions of "turns" could then be revised to "cycles" (e.g. "first 6 turns" => "first 6 cycles"), except in "Input for each turn", "valid actions this turn" and "Output for each turn", which should remain unchanged.

  • "Maximum 2 jumps between two PASS actions" could then be simplified to "Maximum 2 jumps in a cycle".

  • PASS: "Ends the turn." could be clarified to "Ends the current cycle and starts a new cycle."

Rules - Map
* Please state the height and width of the map.

* Please change "The map" to "In every test case, the map".

Rules - Glyphs / flames spawning
* Please end the section with "of that same turn", and move the remainder of the explanation to "Rules - End of turn (PASS)". This would keep this section focused on spawning, while the details of glyph conversion would sit naturally under the PASS section.

Rules - Available actions
* PASS: Please remove the second pair of brackets, as the information inside the brackets is more than just supplementary.

* PASS: Consider adding "(for the new cycle)" after "new glyphs" for clarity.

Rules - Effects of a movement (MOVE or JUMP)
* Point 4 - "increasing distance": Please specify the distance metric (e.g. Euclidean, Manhattan).

* Since the cardinal push and diagonal push examples also illustrate the attraction consequence, consider moving them after point 4, where attraction is explained.

* In the diagonal push and diagonal attraction examples, the two FREE CELL labels near the corner don't seem relevant. Should the top-right corner be labelled FREE CELL instead?

Rules - Effects of IMMO
* Consider replacing "the character's cell blocks flames (just like a wall or another flame)" with "the character's cell behaves like a wall for flames".

Cases
* Consider removing the "Flames Extinguisher - " prefix from every case name. It feels repetitive and the prefix is long enough that the subsequent part of the case name becomes hidden.

* Consider varying the starting positions between cases if they're the same in all the cases.

* Each test and its validator should cover the same scenario but with slightly different inputs. Please disable the validator flag for tests, and enable the validator cases.

Viewer
* Consider doubling or tripling the font size of the information at the top-left corner.

(Play-testing is still on my to-do list.)

Thanks.

I removed the "active" words I forgot.

I added a definition of turn (CodinGame) vs cycle (character's turn) and used these words accordingly.

In the map section is now stated it is 35x34

"Glyphs / Flames spawning" section was renamed "Glyphs spawning". The end of the paragraph was removed from there and the information "Wether or not a flame appears, the glyph disappears" has been moved in the "End of cycle" section.

Useless brackets removed.

Distance is qualified as Manhattan's one.

I added specific sentences for diagonal attraction/push. In fact, a flame needs 3 cells to be open in order to move diagonally :
- The destination
- The two orthogonally adjacent cells on the trajectory of the move. Example : to move NE, the N cell and the E cell must be free too.

I updated the illustrations to include more relevant "FREE CELL".

Updated the IMMO section with "the character's cell behaves like a wall for flames"

Removed the prefix from every test cases.

The starting position never vary on Dofus for this fight, only the glyphs' spawning.

I had an error on parsing the hidden validators. Now there are 10 tests that are also used as validators, and 10 validators (not visible as tests).

I updated the viewer to :
- increase the font size
- add the "jumps left" information

The statement has become much clearer overall. I went through the latest version and also did a little play-testing. Below are some suggestions: Goal * Consider removing "during the first 6 cycles" in the introduction to avoid repetition and going into the details too early. Rules - Map * Please add "in terms of character starting position, wall layout and dimensions" between "the same" and "(35x34)" for clarity. Rules - Glyphs spawning * Consider moving the section after "Turns and cycles", so the explanation flows from "what a cycle is" to "glyphs appearing at the start of a cycle". Rules - Turns and cycles * The definition of a cycle is slightly inaccurate because the first cycle doesn't occur between two consecutive PASS actions (there's no preceding PASS). Also, technically a turn could contain more than one PASS (although additional ones are ignored). So, consider replacing the explanation from the second sentence onwards with: A cycle is the sequence of actions ending with a PASS. A cycle spans one or more turns, up to and including the turn in which a PASS is sent. In other words, all actions in a turn belong to only one cycle. Rules - Available actions * Consider removing "(which resets for the character's next cycle)." as the meaning is already clear without this note. * In "new glyphs appear or, if there are none…", "none" may be misread as referring to "no new glyphs for the next cycle". Based on the "End of cycle (PASS)" section, "none" actually refers to "no glyphs for the current cycle". Since glyphs spawning happens at the start of a cycle and has been described in its own section, consider removing "and new glyphs appear or" to avoid the said potential misunderstanding. * Please add a paragraph at the end of this section to clarify the action limits within a cycle: You may send any number of MOVE and IMMO during a cycle, subject to AP availability. Rules - Effects of a movement (MOVE or JUMP) * Consider adding "(the axis with the greater absolute distance)" after "dominant axis". * Illustrative examples: Please place "cardinal push" and "diagonal push" before "cardinal attraction" and "diagonal attraction" to match the order in which the mechanics are explained in the text. Rules - Effects of IMMO * Consider replacing "he can target" with "the possible target positions are". Inputs for each turn * Line 2: Consider replacing "before the next PASS" with "this cycle" to be more direct. Cases * When clicking PREVIEW or TEST IN IDE, 30 cases appear. This likely means the validator flags haven't been set to false for the tests. Please check and fix this. (character limit almost reached…)

(…continued)

Viewer
* Consider adding a viewer option that displays the coordinates of flames, glyphs and the character on hover. (For reference, the puzzle Battleship Solitaire has an option called "SHOW COORDINATES ON HOVER" ( https://www.codingame.com/ide/puzzle/battleship-solitaire . The page also contains the link to the source code (written in Kotlin) in case you want to study it.)

Referee
* In the warning message "actions after PASS were ignored", please add "the first" before "PASS".

* Consider displaying additional information in the console:

  • The results of every valid action, such as character movement, flame extinguished and glyph conversion.

  • The specific reason for the character's death to replace the text "HP = 0", especially since the viewer sometimes shows negative HP values after death.

* Could you share the list of commands that extinguish all the flames in Test 1? I'd like to verify that the referee behaves correctly for the various actions and in a victory scenario.

Thanks.

I updated the statement with your proposals.

I added a toggle option that shows cell's informations on hover.

I also added gameSummary informations about the actions performed by the player.

I also updated the defeat message to give more on insight on why the character died.

Here is a hardcoded list you can use that beats test 1 with 19 hp left, computed by my own solver (note that the last PASS is not played because the game is already over, but is still here for safety):
private static final List<String> HARDCODED_TEST1 = List.of(
        "PASS",
        "MOVE N",
        "MOVE N",
        "MOVE W",
        "JUMP W",
        "MOVE N",
        "JUMP E",
        "MOVE N",
        "MOVE S",
        "PASS",
        "JUMP N",
        "MOVE W",
        "JUMP E",
        "MOVE S",
        "MOVE W",
        "MOVE N",
        "MOVE E",
        "PASS",
        "JUMP N",
        "MOVE E",
        "MOVE S",
        "JUMP E",
        "MOVE S",
        "MOVE S",
        "MOVE E",
        "PASS",
        "MOVE S",
        "MOVE S",
        "JUMP N",
        "MOVE W",
        "MOVE S",
        "MOVE E",
        "MOVE S",
        "MOVE W",
        "MOVE S",
        "PASS",
        "MOVE E",
        "MOVE S",
        "JUMP S",
        "MOVE W",
        "MOVE N",
        "MOVE N",
        "MOVE W",
        "MOVE N",
        "MOVE W",
        "PASS",
        "MOVE N",
        "JUMP N",
        "MOVE W",
        "MOVE S",
        "MOVE S",
        "MOVE W",
        "MOVE N",
        "MOVE E",
        "MOVE S",
        "PASS",
        "MOVE S",
        "JUMP S",
        "MOVE E",
        "MOVE W",
        "MOVE N",
        "PASS"
    );

You can also use the ";" to send several actions in the same turn, using this list, the result will be the same but with way less CodinGame turns :
private static final List<String> HARDCODED_TEST1 = List.of(
        "PASS",
        "MOVE N;MOVE N;MOVE W;JUMP W;MOVE N;JUMP E;MOVE N;MOVE S;PASS",
        "JUMP N;MOVE W;JUMP E;MOVE S;MOVE W;MOVE N;MOVE E;PASS",
        "JUMP N;MOVE E;MOVE S;JUMP E;MOVE S;MOVE S;MOVE E;PASS",
        "MOVE S;MOVE S;JUMP N;MOVE W;MOVE S;MOVE E;MOVE S;MOVE W;MOVE S;PASS",
        "MOVE E;MOVE S;JUMP S;MOVE W;MOVE N;MOVE N;MOVE W;MOVE N;MOVE W;PASS",
        "MOVE N;JUMP N;MOVE W;MOVE S;MOVE S;MOVE W;MOVE N;MOVE E;MOVE S;PASS",
        "MOVE S;JUMP S;MOVE E;MOVE W;MOVE N"
    );

Thanks for the update and providing the list of commands for passing Test 1. It's very useful.

Rules - Available actions
* PASS: After "flames", please add ", provided its cell is not occupied by the character or another flame. Whether or not a flame appears, the glyph disappears." This explanation was present in the previous version and should be kept.

* Please add a blank line after "availability".

Initialization input - Line 1
* Please add "(always <const>34</const>)" after "height".

* Please add "(always <const>35</const>)" after "width".

Constraints
* Last two constraints: Please replace both "<" with "≤".

Viewer
* Please fix the typo: "INFOS" => "INFO" in the option "CELL INFOS ON HOVER".

* Consider adding a short note in the statement about the display options (see example here: https://www.codingame.com/ide/puzzle/entanglement---episode-1 : "Click the gear icon in the viewer to access display options.")

Referee
* Consider simplifying the console message "Glyphs that could not convert to flames" => "Glyphs not converted to flames".

* Please correct the console message for an invalid jump: use "this cycle" instead of "this turn".

* The message "the character used an action without enough HP left" is a bit ambiguous because it suggests insufficient HP to perform the action. However, the same message is also shown when the character technically had enough HP for the action but dies because reaching 0 HP is fatal. Consider revising the message to "The character used an action that reduced HP to 0 or below."

* When the character moves or jumps and extinguishes a flame, the console reports the flame being extinguished but does not mention the character's movement. For clarity and completeness, can the movement also be reported on a separate line, similar to other movement messages?

Thanks again.

P.S. After walking through a victory solution once, it feels like this puzzle is an optimisation version of the existing (non-optimisation) puzzle, "Power of Thor - Episode 2" ( https://www.codingame.com/training/hard/power-of-thor-episode-2 ). Interesting!

P.P.S. I'm curious about the single non-walkable cell inside the grid near the right edge. Is that part of the design in the original game as well?
